body.u: It was in the year 1978 that I left school. I was 16 years of age and had my job already lined up. I was going in to the naveynavy as a junior seamonseaman operator 2nd class. The training went on in barrack until I was 16 ½ we had had six months training on land and now was our chance to go to sea on the training ships for a further 1 ½ years.

It was after this 2 years that I was made a seamonseaman operator 1st class. I had signed up for 12 years and had ten left to serve. It was a good life and four years later I got my first real promotion. I had in my four years traveledtravelled to a great number of places and was about due to set off for a six month stay in America. When I recievedreceived a distressing letter from my mum.

She had written to tell me that my younger brother mark had been knocked down by a bus and, was lying in hospital in a coma. I went to my captain and asked his permitionpermission for a time off on leave. This was granted and the next day I was on the train home. My brother took six days to come out of his comecoma and I had to fly out to America and pick up my ship from there. The next few years went past quite uninterupteduninterrupted and, I got another promotion I had one year left to serve and decided there and then that I would sign up for the next twelve and get my pentionpension.

Between finishing my first period at work and signing for my next I had two month ofoff. In this time I went around and visited all my old friends. One of whomewhom was called Stephen. During this time we went fishing a great deal and, met some of our old girl friends. It was while we were in a pub that we decided when I finished my next stint in the NaveyNavy we would get together and buy ourselves a charter boat. There was to be no stopping this plan, and while I was a way Stephen got every thing settled.

Two years into my stint I got married to a lass called Jill. We decided the best thing we could do was get ourselves a house with a mortgage no that at least we would have a place of our own. We did not find this too hard as over the years I had saved quite a today sum.

This arrangement went on very well for a good few years we had now got two children both boys with only two years to go until I came out of the naveynavy. I heard from Stephen that he had found the right boarbuyer and paid the down payment.

After this news came to me my last two years seemed to fly past. Within no time atall I was out. I had been given a pentionpension of thirty pounds a week and a lump sum of eight thousand to get my self started on. It was hard work for the first could of years and this money was needed.

We had to make a good name for ourselves as there was a lot of competition in this field. As we had both been keen fishermen all of our lives we did not find this two hard and in our firth year we had no difficulties at all in filling our timtablestimetables. This life was to suit me for the next thirty years of my life and now at the age of sixty eight I have retired still a fit man.
